name: Josh Whyle
headline: Buried behind Musgrave with no route share to dream on
body:
Whyle's profile peaked in 2024 with Tennessee, when he turned 37 targets into a 7.3% target share across 15 games, posting 28 catches for 248 yards, 1.33 yards per route run, and 32.8 fantasy points on a healthy 0.215 EPA per play. That hinted at a usable streaming floor. The move to Green Bay erased it. In 2025 he played just six games, and despite a respectable 39.9% snap rate his route participation cratered to 14.7%, yielding six targets, a 1.3% target share, and only 36 yards. The snaps are blocking reps, not passing-game volume.

The path forward is blocked too. He sits behind Luke Musgrave on the depth chart and carries an afterthought TE109 expert rank with essentially no roster ownership. The 6-foot-7 frame and a career red-zone history keep him on touchdown-vulture watch, but without a meaningful uptick in routes he has no standalone fantasy value in 2026.
